title: Indexing Pitfalls
content:
# DBMS Interview Prep

## Q1: Why does ARIES write Compensation Log Records (CLRs) during the Undo phase?

### Standard Answer
During crash recovery, the database can crash again during the Undo phase. If we did not write CLRs, we would re-undo the same updates multiple times, which violates undo idempotency and causes recovery loops. A CLR logs the fact that an undo operation has occurred and contains an `UndoNextLSN` field pointing to the next LSN to undo. When repeating recovery, the Undo phase reads the CLR and skips directly to the `UndoNextLSN` of the active transaction.


## Q2: What is the spatial complexity bounds of Indexing Pitfalls?

### Standard Answer
The spatial complexity scales strictly as $O(1)$ auxiliary memory unless additional arrays or dynamic heap nodes are allocated, yielding $O(N)$ total space.
